response.justification: The candidate is an experienced senior full-stack software developer with strong web, testing, and some low-latency systems background, but the role requires deep hardware-level expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in the resume. While he meets the years-of-experience bar with a BS in CS, his career trajectory (web platforms, pricing systems, GIS tools) is fundamentally misaligned with a Principal Performance Architect position focused on deep-learning hardware optimization.
strengths: 18+ years of software engineering experience, exceeding the BS + 8 years requirement
Low-latency systems work in DLang (async I/O, fibers, caching) at millions of requests/second scale
Some embedded and hardware-adjacent exposure (robotic arms, LabWindows/CVI, audio DSP thesis)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware performance modeling experience
No AI/deep-learning workload, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD optimization background
Career is web/application focused; a principal-level hardware architecture role is a large domain leap

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
No AI accelerator or DNN workload optimization background
Strong general engineering seniority and low-latency systems skills
